name: Strava MCP Server (via HAPI)
what_it_does: MCP server for Strava fitness platform — enabling AI agents to access athletes, activities, segments, clubs, and routes. Built via the HAPI MCP framework wrapping the Strava API. Enables fitness analysis agents to retrieve workout data, performance metrics, and social fitness features.
use_cases: Analyzing Strava activity history and performance trends from fitness coaching agents
Retrieving athlete activity stats for personalized training recommendations
Monitoring segment and route data for athletic performance agents
Building fitness analytics dashboards using Strava data in AI workflows
not_for: Bulk data exports for commercial fitness products (Strava API terms)
best_when: A fitness enthusiast or coach wants AI agents to analyze Strava workout data — identifying trends, comparing performances, and generating training insights from activity history.
avoid_when: You don't use Strava. Strava API requires OAuth and has strict commercial use restrictions. Health data from fitness activities is sensitive.

auth.notes: Strava OAuth 2.0. Athlete must authorize access. Use read-only scopes for analytics. activity:read for basic; activity:read_all for private activities.

pricing.paid_tiers: None for API; Strava Summit subscription for premium features
pricing.notes: Strava API free for approved apps. Rate limits apply. MCP server is free open source. Commercial use requires Strava API agreement compliance.

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: Strava data includes GPS routes which can reveal home/work locations
OAuth token refresh required for long-running fitness analysis agents
Strict rate limits (100 req/15min) — batch-heavy agents will be throttled
Private activities and hide-segments features must be respected
HAPI framework intermediary — validate that the implementation matches current Strava API
agent_readiness.security_notes: PRIVACY: GPS activity data reveals precise location history including home, work, and frequent routes. OAuth with minimal scopes. Private activities must be respected.

security_score_components.security_notes: GPS location data is highly sensitive — reveals precise movement patterns. OAuth with read-only scopes. GDPR compliance for EU athletes.
